evals.name: diagnose-blocky-town-map
evals.prompt: Our town map in this game looks awful — the roads are just flat tan rectangles slapped onto the grass with hard square edges, like a checkerboard. The whole village reads as unfinished. Can you figure out exactly why it looks like that and tell me what it'd take to make it look like a real, cohesive village? Be concrete about the tiles and the approach.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the root cause (a single flat tile stamped in bands, no autotiling / no transition tiles), explains that terrains must be bridged with edge/corner transition tiles chosen by an autotiler, inspects the actual tileset (montage) and finds the unused grass-sand transition blob, names the files to change (TownScene/townMaps/townLayout), and proposes a concrete plan. Bonus: notes the flat-fill is a beach/sand tile, the 1-wide-road / width pitfall, and that the fix lands on every settlement at once.

evals.name: road-over-grass-and-sand-transitions
evals.prompt: I'm building a top-down tile map (grass base) and adding a dirt road. Where the road runs over grass it looks okay, but where it crosses a sandy beach area the edges look totally wrong. I'm confused about what tiles I actually need. How do transitions work here and how should I lay this out so it looks right everywhere?
evals.expected_output: Explains that transition tiles are PER PAIR of terrains — a road-over-grass set won't work where road meets sand, so road-over-sand needs its own set. Recommends an autotiler driven by a neighbour bitmask, and ideally surfaces the neutral-intermediate trick (give the road a thin dirt shoulder so it only ever transitions to dirt, and dirt transitions to grass/sand — collapsing the number of sets needed). Mentions fill + edges + outer corners + inner corners and verifying inner corners/junctions.

evals.name: generate-missing-shoreline
evals.prompt: My grass and water tiles meet in a hard square border so my lakes look like rectangles. The tileset has grass tiles and water tiles but nothing for the shoreline in between. How do I make the shoreline tiles and wire them up so the lake edges look natural?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the missing transition SET (grass-to-water shore) is the problem, explains you must author/generate it rather than butt the fills, recommends a scheme (8-bit blob, or dual-grid 16-tile corner method to halve the art), describes what a shore tile is (water fill feathering into grass on the relevant sides, with matching light/palette), points to a generation path (pixel-art-craft hand-authoring or pixellab tileset, noting async), and an autotiler to place them by neighbour bitmask.
